content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL p RACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 958 I 4-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Paul Colin 
1702-L Meridian Ave., #195 
San Jose, CA 95125-5586 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-12-028 
Dear Mr. Colin: 
February 24, 2012 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 You are a deputy district attorney for Santa Clara County. 
You have requested advice regarding mailing letters from your campaign to county employees. 
The law that applies to your question is Government Code Section 3025, which is not included 
within the Act. The Fair Political Practices Commission ("the Commission") can only advise on 
the provisions of the Act. We therefore cannot respond to your inquiry and are withdrawing 
your request. For your reference, there is a campaign manual on our website that has 
information for those running for Superior Court judgeships. You can find it here: 
http://www.fppc.ca.gov/manuals/manual2local. pdf 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
HMR:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
Heather M. Rowan 
Counsel, Legal Division 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.question: Can a deputy district attorney for Santa Clara County mail letters from their campaign to county employees?
sections.conclusion: The law that applies to your question is Government Code Section 3025, which is not included within the Act. The Fair Political Practices Commission ("the Commission") can only advise on the provisions of the Act. We therefore cannot respond to your inquiry and are withdrawing your request.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor, a deputy district attorney running for Superior Court judge, sought advice on whether they could mail campaign letters to county employees.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the request because the applicable law (Government Code Section 3025) falls outside the Political Reform Act's scope, which is the only authority the Commission can advise upon.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter (W-12-028). No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections with standard headers, but the question and conclusion are clearly stated in the body text. The document references a campaign manual as an alternative resource for the requestor.

CONCLUSION: The law that applies to your question is Government Code Section 3025, which is not included within the Act. The Fair Political Practices Commission ("the Commission") can only advise on the provisions of the Act. We therefore cannot respond to your inquiry and are withdrawing your request.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew a request for advice from a deputy district attorney regarding campaign mailings to county employees because the applicable law (Government Code Section 3025) is outside the Commission's jurisdiction under the Political Reform Act.
